title: crepis bakeri var. cusickii
intro: Crepis bakeri var. cusickii, also known as Cusick's hawksbeard, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the western United States. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections. Crepis bakeri var. cusickii can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal benefits, and its leaves and roots can be used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to soothe and heal skin irritations.
